description: An issue was discovered on Eminent EM4544 9.10 devices. The device does not require the user's current password to set a new one within the web interface. Therefore, it is possible to exploit this issue (e.g., in combination with a successful XSS, or at an unattended workstation) to change the admin password to an attacker-chosen value without knowing the current password.
Claude Reasoning: The description never states what kind of device the Eminent EM4544 is; it only describes a generic web-interface password-change weakness, and the CPE is an application CPE with no product wording. Without a device type there is no way to tell whether this is a home alarm system or unrelated consumer networking hardware.
